text.title: TTS Basics — Output Contract
text.summary: JSON shape returned by a single synthesize() call. Carries the saved audio path, duration estimate, provider, voice, cache-hit flag, and a cache key for downstream replay.

section.title: JSON Schema
section.code: { "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema", "$id": "faion://tts-basics/output.schema.json", "type": "object", "required": ["status", "path", "duration_s", "provider", "voice", "model", "cache_key", "cached"], "properties": { "status": {"type": "string", "enum": ["ok", "error"]}, "path": {"type": "string", "minLength": 1, "description": "absolute or repo-relative filesystem path"}, "duration_s": {"type": "number", "minimum": 0.0}, "provider": {"type": "string", "enum": ["openai", "elevenlabs", "google", "azure"]}, "voice": {"type": "string", "minLength": 1}, "model": {"type": "string", "minLength": 1}, "speed": {"type": "number", "minimum": 0.25, "maximum": 4.0, "default": 1.0}, "cache_key": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[a-f0-9]{64}$"}, "cached": {"type": "boolean"}, "error": {"type": "string", "description": "present only when status == 'error'"} }, "additionalProperties": false }

list.item: status — "ok" on saved audio file, "error" on provider failure or invalid input.
path — non-empty filesystem path; downstream callers MUST verify it exists before reading.
duration_s — measured via pydub or ffprobe; 0.0 is treated as an error by callers.
provider — one of openai / elevenlabs / google / azure (no provider-specific synonyms).
cached — true iff the audio file was served from cache and no provider call was made.

antipattern.description: cache_key computed without provider in the input string.
antipattern.reason: Cross-provider cache collisions return wrong-voice audio for identical text.
0-second reported duration is almost always an empty or invalid audio file; treat as error.

antipattern.description: voice is a semantic label ("news", "narrator") rather than the provider-native id.
antipattern.reason: Downstream replay cannot reconstruct the exact synthesis without the native id; semantic labels are router-side only.

example.note: Invalid: empty path, 0.0 duration with status ok, semantic voice label instead of native id, cache_key not sha256.

list.item: path exists on disk and file size > 1024 bytes.
provider value is one of the four enum members.
voice value matches the provider's documented voice id (not a semantic label).
